meta.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept CPBS items for SpeedyCAT (chunk 6 of 10). NOT real MCAT/AAMC content and NOT from any answer key. Every stem and choice is plain text (no HTML/SVG/LaTeX). Answer keys were computed programmatically and each item passed a 4-criteria eval (four distinct choices; clean plain-text formatting; MCAT-appropriate difficulty; exactly one correct choice).

questions.stem: A current of 1 A flows through a 20 Ω resistor. How much power is dissipated?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A wave has a period of 0.05 s. What is its frequency?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of PCl3?
questions.explanation: PCl3 adopts a trigonal pyramidal geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many molecules are in 2 mol of a substance? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: molecules = moles x N_A = 2 x 6.022 x 10^23 = 1.2 x 10^24.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the energy of a photon of frequency 1 x 10^14 Hz? (h = 6.63 x 10^-34 J·s)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the approximate bond angle in PCl3 (trigonal pyramidal)?
questions.explanation: A trigonal pyramidal molecule such as PCl3 has bond angles of about 107 degrees.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For the reaction 4 Al + 3 O2 → 2 Al2O3, how many moles of Al2O3 form from 8 mol of Al (with the other reactant in excess)?
questions.explanation: Use the mole ratio 2 Al2O3 : 4 Al: 8 x 2/4 = 4 mol Al2O3.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the pH of a solution with [H+] = 1 x 10^-6 M?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much heat is required to melt (fuse) 25 g of water? (latent heat = 334 J/g)
questions.explanation: q = m L = (25)(334) = 8350 J for the phase change (no temperature change).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 1 m/s through a pipe of cross-section 10 cm^2. What is its speed where the cross-section narrows to 1 cm^2?
questions.explanation: Continuity: A1 v1 = A2 v2, so v2 = (10)(1)/1 = 10 m/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What ionic charge do elements in group 13 most commonly form?
questions.explanation: Elements in group 13 typically form ions with a charge of +3.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the weight of a 1 kg object near Earth's surface? (g = 9.8 m/s^2)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Resistors of 2 Ω, 3 Ω, 5 Ω are connected in series. What is the total resistance?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the energy of a photon of wavelength 1 x 10^-6 m? (h = 6.63 x 10^-34 J·s, c = 3.0 x 10^8 m/s)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the hybridization of the central atom in BeCl2?
questions.explanation: The electron-domain geometry of BeCl2 corresponds to sp hybridization of the central atom.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the mass percent of O in CaCO3?
questions.explanation: mass percent = (mass of element / molar mass) x 100 = (48/100) x 100 = 48%.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: At 25 degrees C, a solution has pH = 9. What is its pOH?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = 300 kJ and ΔH2 = 100 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = 300 + 100 = 400 kJ.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 3 m/s through a pipe of cross-sectional area 2 m^2. What is the volume flow rate?
questions.explanation: Flow rate Q = A v = (2)(3) = 6 m^3/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the atomic number of neon?
questions.explanation: The atomic number equals the number of protons; neon has 10.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many valence electrons does a neutral chlorine (Cl) atom have?
questions.explanation: chlorine (Cl) is in a main group whose number of valence electrons is 7.

questions.stem: A sample has mass 8 g and volume 50 mL. What is its density?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Using PV = nRT (R = 0.0821 L·atm/(mol·K)), what is the pressure of 0.5 mol of gas in 1 L at 400 K?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For a reaction, ΔH = -100 kJ and ΔS = 0.05 kJ/K. What is ΔG at 300 K?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which state of a given substance generally has the highest entropy?
questions.explanation: Gas particles are most disordered and spread out, giving the highest entropy.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the ground-state electron configuration of a neutral neon (Z=10) atom?
questions.explanation: Filling orbitals by the Aufbau principle gives neon: 1s2 2s2 2p6.

questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object is 60 cm from a thin converging lens of focal length 30 cm. Where is the image formed?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The bond in MgO has an electronegativity difference of about 2.3. It is best classified as
questions.explanation: An electronegativity difference near 2.3 indicates a ionic bond (roughly: 0 nonpolar covalent, up to about 1.7 polar covalent, larger values ionic).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Convert 750 nm to meters.
questions.explanation: Apply the SI prefix conversion: 750 nm = 7.5 x 10^-7 m.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas at 3 atm occupies 6 L. At constant temperature, what volume does it occupy at 0.5 atm?
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: P1 V1 = P2 V2, so V2 = (3)(6)/0.5 = 36 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to Le Chatelier's principle, increasing the pressure by decreasing volume of a gaseous a system at equilibrium generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

choices.label: B
choices.text: shifts toward the side with fewer moles of gas.

choices.label: C
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.

choices.label: D
choices.text: shifts toward the side with more moles of gas.

questions.explanation: The system opposes the change: increasing the pressure by decreasing volume of a gaseous a system at equilibrium shifts toward the side with fewer moles of gas.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A system absorbs 1000 J of heat and does 100 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 1000 - 100 = 900 J.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the larger atomic radius, O or S?

choices.label: C
choices.text: atomic radius shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: S has the larger atomic radius: same group; radius increases down a group, so S is larger.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the gravitational potential energy of a 0.5 kg object raised 3 m? (g = 9.8 m/s^2)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Two parallel plates 0.03 m apart have a potential difference of 9 V. What is the electric field between them?
questions.explanation: For parallel plates, E = V / d = 9 / 0.03 = 300 V/m.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For a lens, the object distance is 10 cm and the image distance is 5 cm. What is the magnification?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Overall, the molecule O2 is best described as
questions.explanation: O2 is a nonpolar molecule because its symmetric shape makes the bond dipoles cancel, giving no net dipole.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: In the reaction Zn + 2 HCl → ZnCl2 + H2, this is a
questions.explanation: One element (Zn) replaces another (H) in a compound: a single-replacement reaction.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas occupies 10 L at 200 K. At constant pressure, what volume does it occupy at 400 K?
questions.explanation: Charles's law: V1/T1 = V2/T2, so V2 = 10 x 400/200 = 20 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A large equilibrium constant (K much greater than 1) indicates that at equilibrium,
questions.explanation: K much greater than 1 means product concentrations dominate.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas expands by 1 x 10^-3 m^3 against a constant pressure of 1.5 x 10^5 Pa. How much work does it do?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Across a period (left to right), first ionization energy generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: drops to zero at the halogens.

questions.explanation: Stronger nuclear attraction makes electrons harder to remove across a period.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much power is delivered if 100 J of work is done in 8 s?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much energy is stored in a 100 μF capacitor charged to 10 V?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: After 5 half-lives, what fraction of a radioactive sample remains?
questions.explanation: Each half-life halves the amount: fraction = (1/2)^5 = 0.0312.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the strongest type of intermolecular force present in pure Ne?
questions.explanation: The strongest intermolecular force in Ne is London dispersion forces.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the approximate molar mass of O2? (Use H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Na=23, Cl=35.5, S=32, K=39, Ca=40, Mg=24, Fe=56, Ag=108)
questions.explanation: Summing the atomic masses in O2 gives a molar mass of about 32 g/mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What volume does 3 mol of an ideal gas occupy at STP? (molar volume = 22.4 L/mol)
questions.explanation: V = moles x 22.4 L/mol = 3 x 22.4 = 67.2 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much work is required to move a charge of 3 C through a potential difference of 12 V?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A sample starts with 200 g of a radioisotope whose half-life is 5 days. How much remains after 5 days?
questions.explanation: Number of half-lives = 5/5 = 1; remaining = 200 x (1/2)^1 = 100 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A sigma bond is formed by

choices.label: A
choices.text: head-on (end-to-end) overlap of orbitals along the internuclear axis.

choices.label: B
choices.text: overlap of two fully filled orbitals.

choices.label: D
choices.text: side-by-side overlap of parallel p orbitals.

questions.explanation: Sigma bonds arise from axial overlap; pi bonds from sideways p-orbital overlap.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many moles are in 8 g of CH4? (molar mass 16 g/mol)

questions.explanation: pH above 7 indicates a basic (alkaline) solution.

questions.stem: Adding 8360 J of heat to 100 g of water raises its temperature by how much? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))

questions.stem: Convert 50 degrees C to kelvin. (Use K = C + 273.)

questions.stem: How many protons are in an atom of nitrogen-14?
questions.explanation: The number of protons equals the atomic number; for nitrogen-14 that is 7.

questions.stem: An object moves in a circle of radius 10 m at 2 m/s. What is its centripetal acceleration?

questions.stem: In a series circuit, which quantity is the same through every element?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The power dissipated by each element.

questions.explanation: In series there is one path, so the same current flows through all elements; voltages add.

questions.stem: As the wavelength of a wave increases (at fixed speed), its frequency
questions.explanation: Since v = f λ at fixed v, frequency and wavelength are inversely related.

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of H2S?
questions.explanation: H2S adopts a bent geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = -400 kJ and ΔH2 = 100 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?

questions.stem: How do mass and weight differ?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Weight is always constant but mass changes with location.

choices.label: B
choices.text: They are identical quantities measured in different units.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Mass measures the amount of matter (kg); weight is the gravitational force on that mass (N).

choices.label: D
choices.text: Mass is a force and weight is a quantity of matter.

questions.explanation: Mass is an intrinsic scalar in kilograms; weight = m g is a force in newtons that depends on local g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
